name: AgentAIMO Phase 4 — Competition Prep
description: AIMO 3 math solver. Deadline April 8 2026. All tests must stay green. Use Codex (o4-mini) for routine tasks, Claude Code (opus) for complex D-tier reasoning. Run `npx gitnexus analyze` after every commit. Run tests with `python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q`.

userStories.title: Swap majority vote for AnswerSelector in kaggle_notebook.py
userStories.description: Replace the Counter/majority_vote logic in notebook/kaggle_notebook.py with src/solver/answer_selector.AnswerSelector.select(). Build minimal VerificationReport objects (ConfidenceLevel.NL_JUDGMENT) for each attempt where real reports are unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: Counter / majority_vote pattern is removed or replaced
python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q passes with no new failures
userStories.completionNotes: Completed by Claude Sonnet session 2026-03-21. Swapped Counter with AnswerSelector in deep_researcher.py and added Cell 7 to notebook.

userStories.title: Add prompt reinforcement for top flaw codes in agent/prompts.py
userStories.description: Add 3 instruction phrases to the system/user prompt in agent/prompts.py to address CONTEXT_CONFABULATION, MISSING_FINAL_COMMIT, and CHANNEL_LEAKAGE.
acceptanceCriteria: agent/prompts.py includes mandatory **ANSWER: N** format instruction
userStories.completionNotes: Completed by Claude Sonnet session 2026-03-21. Added 3 sections to SYSTEM_PROMPT.

userStories.title: Implement select_two() two-attempt policy in AnswerSelector
userStories.description: Add select_two() method to src/solver/answer_selector.py. Returns (best, second_best_disagreeing). 3 tests added to tests/test_phase3.py.

userStories.title: Integrate select_two into notebook for 2-attempt submission policy
userStories.description: Update notebook/kaggle_notebook.py Cell 7 to use select_two(). Attempt 1 = highest-confidence, Attempt 2 = highest-confidence disagreeing. Fallback: use first for both.
acceptanceCriteria: Notebook calls select_two() instead of select()
Graceful fallback when second answer is None

userStories.title: Fix 15 pre-existing test failures in test_verification_battery.py
userStories.description: There are 15 pre-existing failures in tests/test_verification_battery.py (arithmetic_checker and symbolic_checker tests). These have been noted since the Sonnet session but never investigated. Fix them — the test suite should be fully green, not 'green with known failures'.
acceptanceCriteria: python -m pytest tests/test_verification_battery.py -v passes with 0 failures
No tests removed or skipped to achieve this
userStories.notes: Agent: Codex. Read the failing tests first, then read the checker implementations in src/verification/. The failures are likely in arithmetic_checker.py and symbolic_checker.py.

userStories.title: Run ablation with real Kaggle traces and document extract_rate
userStories.description: PREVIOUS ATTEMPT FAILED: Sonnet session marked this done but ablation_extraction.py skipped because raw JSONL files (data/research/research_data*.jsonl) don't exist locally. This task requires a live Kaggle run first OR finding the raw trace files. Investigate whether trace data exists elsewhere in the repo or needs to be regenerated.
acceptanceCriteria: Ablation script actually runs on real data (not just writing a doc saying 'data missing')
Extract rate result is documented clearly
userStories.notes: Agent: Codex. Check data/verification/audit_research_data*.jsonl — these exist. If raw traces are truly missing, compute extract rates from audit files as a legitimate fallback, but document what was actually measured vs what requires a Kaggle run.

userStories.title: Regenerate BASELINE_METRICS.md with real data
userStories.description: PREVIOUS ATTEMPT INCOMPLETE: Sonnet session updated the date but couldn't run run_baseline.py because raw input files are missing. The 3/8 and 6/8 numbers are stale pre-P4 metrics. Need to either: (a) run a new Kaggle inference to generate fresh traces, or (b) clearly document that the >=5/8 target requires new inference and cannot be verified offline.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagnostic note explains exactly what can vs cannot be measured offline
If new inference data is available, run run_baseline.py and report real numbers
userStories.notes: Agent: Codex. This is blocked on having raw trace data. If the data doesn't exist, the honest answer is to document that and move on to tasks that are actionable.

userStories.title: [CLAUDE OPUS] Diagnose why aime_2024_3 and aime_2024_5 have 0 extractions
userStories.description: From BASELINE_METRICS.md: aime_2024_3 (GT=809) and aime_2024_5 (GT=104) have no_answer_extracted with PSEUDO_VERIFICATION flaws. Read raw traces in data/verification/audit_research_data.jsonl. Root cause: solver never arrives at the correct answer, or extraction strips it? This is a diagnostic task — analyze only, do not implement.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause identified: solver failure vs extraction failure
Concrete fix proposed (prompt change, sampler config, or extraction rule change)
userStories.notes: Agent: Claude Opus. This requires reasoning about traces. Run with: --agent claude --model opus --iterations 1

userStories.title: [CLAUDE OPUS] Formalize verification budget allocation algorithm
userStories.description: Read docs/evolution.md. Core open problem: optimal allocation of finite verification compute across N traces and M claims. Formalize as stochastic scheduling. Solve uniform-cost bandit warmup, extend to heterogeneous costs. Produce pseudocode for src/solver/answer_selector.py.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/VERIFICATION_BUDGET_ALGORITHM.md produced with formal problem statement
Full heterogeneous-cost case addressed with concrete algorithm
userStories.notes: Agent: Claude Opus with extended thinking. Run with: --agent claude --model opus --iterations 1

userStories.title: Clean up Safe-tier root clutter per MOVE_CANDIDATES.md
userStories.description: Execute only the Safe tier moves from docs/MOVE_CANDIDATES.md — chat logs, .bak files, noise files to _attic/noise/. Do NOT touch Risky tier or any Python/test/doc files.
acceptanceCriteria: No Python source files moved or deleted
userStories.notes: Agent: Codex. Low priority — only do this when all actionable tasks above are complete.
